diagrams.title: TCP Client-Server Connection Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the sequence of socket API calls between client and server for TCP connection establishment
diagrams.purpose: Understand the order of socket operations and connection establishment

diagrams.title: Three-Way Handshake
diagrams.description: Detailed view of TCP SYN-SYN-ACK-ACK handshake process
elements: Client sends SYN with sequence number x
Server sends SYN-ACK with ack x+1 and sequence y
Client sends ACK with ack y+1
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how reliable TCP connections are established

diagrams.title: TCP vs UDP Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of connection types and data delivery
elements: Header sizes: TCP(20 bytes), UDP(8 bytes)
diagrams.purpose: Understand differences in transport protocols for appropriate selection

diagrams.title: Client-Server Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows multiple clients connecting to a central server with request-response flow
elements: Requests flow from client to server
Responses flow from server to client

visuals.title: Client Server Programming
visuals.description: Visual diagram for client server programming
